source_name: MDN CSS gradients and Apple Liquid Glass material guidance
content:
# MDN CSS gradients and Apple Liquid Glass material guidance - Overview

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium AI dashboard background, Liquid Glass ecological interface, ambient hero background, frosted card backdrop, calm high-end SaaS UI
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/gradient

Visual traits:
- slow aurora bands behind translucent glass
- misty atmospheric haze with soft depth falloff
- green-blue, silver, cyan, and restrained violet light fields
- large low-frequency gradients instead of decorative blobs
- subtle scene movement that remains calmer than the UI

Use when:
- premium AI dashboard background
- Liquid Glass ecological interface
- ambient hero background
- frosted card backdrop
- calm high-end SaaS UI

Avoid when:
- fast rainbow gradient cycling
- high-frequency animated stripes
- bright aurora directly behind form labels
- single purple-blue full-page gradient
- decorative orb backgrounds without scene depth
- marketing hero composition when the task is an operational dashboard

AI summary: Use aurora haze backgrounds for high-end Liquid Glass interfaces that need a calm ecological scene behind glass. Build broad, slow, layered gradient bands with mist, noise, dimming, and reduced-motion fallback; avoid fast rainbow motion, decorative blobs, and bright scenery behind text.

source_name: MDN CSS gradients and Apple Liquid Glass material guidance
content:
# MDN CSS gradients and Apple Liquid Glass material guidance - Layout Rules

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium AI dashboard background, Liquid Glass ecological interface, ambient hero background, frosted card backdrop, calm high-end SaaS UI
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/gradient

- Place aurora motion in the far background and keep the UI layer stable.
- Use broad bands and haze fields; avoid small repeated blobs that look decorative rather than environmental.
- Keep the brightest aurora away from text-heavy cards and forms.
- Pair dark glass cards with dimmed aurora fields, not full-saturation neon.
- Use a subtle noise or grain layer to prevent flat digital gradients.

AI summary: Use aurora haze backgrounds for high-end Liquid Glass interfaces that need a calm ecological scene behind glass. Build broad, slow, layered gradient bands with mist, noise, dimming, and reduced-motion fallback; avoid fast rainbow motion, decorative blobs, and bright scenery behind text.

source_name: MDN CSS gradients and Apple Liquid Glass material guidance
content:
# MDN CSS gradients and Apple Liquid Glass material guidance - Accessibility Notes

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium AI dashboard background, Liquid Glass ecological interface, ambient hero background, frosted card backdrop, calm high-end SaaS UI
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/gradient

- Use prefers-reduced-motion to freeze or greatly slow aurora bands.
- Keep text on tinted glass or solid-enough panels, not directly over bright aurora strokes.
- Avoid rapid hue cycling or high-contrast sweeping bands near reading areas.
- Test contrast on the final composite after backdrop blur and saturation.

AI summary: Use aurora haze backgrounds for high-end Liquid Glass interfaces that need a calm ecological scene behind glass. Build broad, slow, layered gradient bands with mist, noise, dimming, and reduced-motion fallback; avoid fast rainbow motion, decorative blobs, and bright scenery behind text.

source_name: MDN CSS gradients and Apple Liquid Glass material guidance
content:
# MDN CSS gradients and Apple Liquid Glass material guidance - Implementation Hints

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium AI dashboard background, Liquid Glass ecological interface, ambient hero background, frosted card backdrop, calm high-end SaaS UI
Evidence: summary_only / high
Source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/gradient

- CSS-only baseline: layer radial-gradient and linear-gradient backgrounds on fixed pseudo-elements, then animate transform and opacity over 30s to 90s loops.
- Use mask-image or large blurred pseudo-elements to make aurora bands fade into the base field.
- Animate transform, background-position, or opacity rather than expensive full-screen filter changes.
- Use CSS variables for aurora hue, haze opacity, scene dim, and motion speed.
- Add a dark translucent page overlay when cards need stronger text contrast.
- For mobile, reduce opacity and motion amplitude to preserve battery and legibility.

AI summary: Use aurora haze backgrounds for high-end Liquid Glass interfaces that need a calm ecological scene behind glass. Build broad, slow, layered gradient bands with mist, noise, dimming, and reduced-motion fallback; avoid fast rainbow motion, decorative blobs, and bright scenery behind text.
